title: cereus neonesioticus
intro: Cereus neonesioticus, also known as the "Prickly Pear Cactus," is a species of cactus native to the Americas and is a type of columnar cactus that belongs to the Cereus genus. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of flavonoids and betalains, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Cereus neonesioticus include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and providing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's stems and fruits are often used to make jams, jellies, and teas, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
